content.full_text:
December 20, 1978 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
Sacramento, California 
Re: 
Fishkin Campaign Committee 
LD. 
770041 
The current campaign debt of this Committee is less than $1,000.00. 
I plan no further fundraisers but simply to pay it from personal 
funds. 
Any reports (after the one due on January 31, 1979), will 
simply reflect transfer of my personal funds to the campaign account 
and payments on outstanding debts. 
Is there any method for me to file a final report showing this? 
Very truly y~urs, 
JE~OME FISH~rN 
Att=brney ~t Law 
JF/rsg 
Jerome Fishkin, Esq. 
1978 -
45th Avenue 
San Francisco, CA 94116

sections.question: Is there any method for me to file a final report showing this?
sections.question_synthetic: The writer seeks guidance on whether there is a procedure for filing a final campaign report after the January 31, 1979 deadline when only personal fund transfers and debt payments will be made.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from a campaign committee representative to FPPC, not an FPPC response. No CONCLUSION or ANSWER section is present. The letter contains a single direct question about final reporting procedures. Document includes campaign committee ID 770041 and is dated December 20, 1978.

embedding.qa_text: QUESTION: Is there any method for me to file a final report showing this?

embedding.summary: Jerome Fishkin, an attorney representing the Fishkin Campaign Committee with outstanding campaign debt under $1,000, inquires about the proper procedure for filing a final campaign report showing only personal fund transfers and debt repayment with no further fundraising.
